title: Gynoxys tuestae
taxonomy: Gynoxys tuestae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. Within the family Asteraceae, Gynoxys tuestae is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants known for their succulent leaves and small, daisy-like flowers. The genus Gynoxys is also part of the family Asteraceae and contains approximately 200 species, many of which are found in the Andean region of South America. Gynoxys tuestae is native to Ecuador and is a relatively rare species. Further classification of Gynoxys tuestae may involve additional levels, such as genus, species, and subspecies, but it is generally classified as a species within the family Asteraceae.
